title: Correct: dot-stuffing in multiline text: strings
description: WHY THIS IS CORRECT: RFC 5228 §2.3.1 specifies that in a multiline text block, a line beginning with '..' has the first dot removed (dot-stuffing), and a line consisting solely of '.' terminates the block. The lexer checks line_trimmed == '.' for termination before checking for the '..' prefix, so the terminator is never mistakenly stored as content. The strip_prefix('..') correctly removes exactly one leading dot from stuffed lines. Lines starting with a single '.' that are not the terminator are passed through unchanged, which is correct (only '..' is stuffed). EOF without a terminator returns a clean ParseError.
